finding: CVS1000 generic JSON endpoint ignores category_code for the price matrix and falls back to the namecard (CNC1000) matrix. q200=4000/SNW300W00 is byte-identical to CNC1000. The endpoint DOES differentiate other namecard codes (CNC2000=3500, CNC3000=10000), so the CVS1000 collapse to CNC1000 is a genuine fallback, not a global outage.
conclusion: q200=4000 is the namecard base price leaking through, NOT the real invitation-card price. Real CVS1000 pricing (goods_view cascade, OMO-3111) = MOQ 500 sheets, q500 supply 70,500 KRW. Disposition: quote-only (auto-sync excluded, last-good 25000 preserved). MUST NOT be added to MATRIX_VERIFIED_SLUGS because the fallback matrix has a valid-looking MOQ(200) and value(4000 != 64000 sentinel) and would pass all guards.

CVS2000.note: 고급초대장 — no customer slug in CATEGORY_MAP, not synced

productParamInvariance: CVS1000 returns the identical 4000 matrix for product=name|card|est|invitation — the product param does not unlock a CVS-specific matrix.
